112980.explanation: We strive for a council of mayor and aldermen with proportional representation of men and women; the right competencies, knowledge and experience are paramount; full-time availability and involvement with our residents (aldermen who live in the municipality) are also very important to us.

112981.explanation: Participation of residents and neighbors is very important. Through a citizens' assembly, residents have contributed to the 2050 vision, and residents in Biddinghuizen and Swifterbant also participate. A referendum simplifies a complex issue into a yes or no choice without room for nuance.

112984.explanation: Cafés and restaurants may be open until 3:00 a.m. To ensure neighbors can also enjoy their night’s rest, it is good that the municipality supervises terraces to close earlier. Through APV regulations, the municipality can balance the interests of different groups of residents.

112985.explanation: Collaboration within energy hubs is a smart way to counteract grid congestion. Participation and co-financing can be arranged via the Regional Energy Strategy (RES). Residents cooperating in an energy cooperative can claim the area-specific contribution from the Wind Funds.

112986.explanation: Location policy must focus on a future-proof (circular) economy for innovative entrepreneurs. This means investing in energy-efficient facilities, smarter use of existing space – considering people, environment, accessibility, employment, and sustainability.

112987.explanation: As the CDA faction, we already opposed the extension of emergency shelter in 2024! Dronten already does more than enough. We accommodate 1300 asylum seekers at the Kampen reception center. The COA emergency shelter on the festival grounds in Biddinghuizen was intended only as a temporary emergency measure.

112990.explanation: We do not consider a ban a good idea. We want to reduce regulatory pressure on entrepreneurs and SMEs. An awareness campaign similar to keeping shop doors closed in cold weather is more effective and customer-friendly.

112991.explanation: Fireworks and carbide shooting are beautiful traditions related to celebrating the New Year. The municipality designates a suitable location and can organize the fireworks show together with and sponsored by businesses, making it free to attend.

112992.explanation: Sounds attractive but leads to increased waste levies. Waste processing must be cost-covering. Residents without bulky waste also contribute, which is unfair. We do support a weekly collection point in Swifterbant and Biddinghuizen, like for collecting Christmas trees.

112996.explanation: The local public broadcaster receives a small financial contribution per household via the municipal fund. Additionally, other local media provide valuable journalistic contributions to news dissemination and reporting. Variety of independent media is good for democracy.

113002.explanation: In cases of serious nuisance, the CDA wants it to be possible and remain possible to deploy (mobile) camera surveillance. Camera surveillance works preventively, contributes to the feeling of safety, and can help in the detection of suspects.

113003.explanation: Sufficient safe shelter and housing for urgent seekers must be available across the Netherlands, including women who are victims of domestic violence. By organizing a walk during the 'Break the Silence' action week as women councillors, we raise awareness of violence against women.

113004.explanation: Aggression in society toward caregivers and also towards enforcement officers (boa's) is increasing. Boa's may be equipped with a baton or pepper spray, but Dronten is not Rotterdam. Smart awareness campaigns like 'without bikes it’s pleasant here' also work well and are customer-friendly.

113006.explanation: OFW must prioritize people who already live, work, or have a strong social bond with the municipality. Because of a shortage, OFW must newly build hundreds of sustainable social rental homes with low energy costs. We want 30% of new construction to be social rental housing.

113009.explanation: There are relatively few vacant offices in Dronten municipality and a shortage of free plots in business parks. Possibly, vacant shops in shopping centers can still host residential units.

113010.explanation: In October 2024, we submitted a motion about the shortage of student housing: by 2027 at the latest, 100 to 200 extra student homes must be realized in suitable locations. Students have the right to proper homes, so no containers.

113011.explanation: Everyone participates according to ability. Residents on welfare have a job search obligation. If circumstances prevent working, there are many opportunities to do meaningful volunteer work. That is good for self-confidence, expands networks, and offers perspective.

113012.explanation: Debt remission is only possible after providing all information about income and expenses. Problems often pile up; this can lead to years of hopeless debt issues. This results in higher social costs than a one-time remission of municipal taxes.

113016.explanation: Within and in residential neighborhoods, 30 km/h is the maximum. This is necessary to allow children to play safely and to prevent noise and vibrations. On through (access) roads, 50 km/h is allowed: an optimal balance between traffic flow and safety.

113019.explanation: Freedom of religion and self-direction are very important to us. Residents should be able to decide how to arrange their lives, including care and guidance. This may mean residents pay for it themselves. It is not fair to ask other residents to contribute to this.

113026.explanation: As the CDA, we strive for a balanced balance between Sunday rest and the interests of our retailers. Opening every Sunday leads to a downward spiral: less liveliness and revenue in shopping centers and high work pressure for retailers.

113027.explanation: Our own residents wait years on a rental housing waitlist. Without priority rules, the reception centers become overcrowded and more expensive temporary emergency shelters are needed. The real solution is to build more social rental and flexible homes for urgent seekers and distribute them fairly.

113029.explanation: Our fertile agricultural land is scarce and needed for food production. First, solar panels should be placed on roofs and then possibilities explored such as covering parking lots, verges, dikes. Agricultural land is a last resort.

113031.explanation: With the accessibility contribution on the Dronten Pass, families can pay (part of) their children's swimming lessons. Schools are not enthusiastic about wet gym lessons; teachers/schools can be held liable if an accident happens during swimming with a child.

113032.explanation: We are against establishing a coffee shop in Dronten. It does not fit a healthy lifestyle for our youth. Cannabis use is also harmful to mentally vulnerable residents. Therefore, we continue the zero-tolerance policy for coffee shops and prostitution.

113034.explanation: We cherish the village character of Dronten. This means only limited space for apartment complexes higher than 4 floors. Deviations are only possible if well-motivated and under special circumstances, subject to the municipal council's decision.

113036.explanation: There is a shortage of parking spaces near shops in Swifterbant center. With the realization of the Kombuis, parking spaces have disappeared, while new homes have been added. In all villages, the number of parking spaces must grow with the population.

113038.explanation: Research shows there is a large shortage of housing for temporary labor migrants in Dronten. The council has designated a limited number of suitable locations on the outskirts and in the Poort van Dronten business park.

113040.explanation: B&W and the municipal council have submitted a position statement against Defense's expansion plans. In the statement, the municipality objected to the expansion of the low-flying area for helicopters above Ketelmeer and north of Dronten.

113041.explanation: The municipality must focus on social effects in all villages, such as meeting and connecting people, combating loneliness, and reducing poverty. This can be done by providing subsidies and collaborating with various social partners, including the Social Domain foundation.

112980.explanation: We choose our aldermen based on content. We would like it if the number of men and women in the council could be made equal.

112981.explanation: For VVD Dronten, it is important to be outside the town hall as much as possible, in neighborhoods and with associations. That way we know what is happening in our municipality. Residents, associations, and businesses must be involved well in advance. A referendum is not needed for this.

112984.explanation: We find a vibrant hospitality sector extremely important for our villages. Entrepreneurs should therefore be able to decide their own opening hours, so they can be open when there is demand!

112985.explanation: The Dronten municipality plays a key role and serves as an example in sustainability. We find it important to cooperate with residents, companies, and other governments to stimulate local energy use and production. This should mainly happen through consultation and permits.

112986.explanation: VVD Dronten likes to promote entrepreneurship and wants to attract new businesses to the municipality and retain existing ones. VVD Dronten does, however, prefer different types of companies than distribution centers. Fortunately, surrounding municipalities already offer a lot of space for that sector.

112987.explanation: Dronten municipality is already called the 'asylum capital.' We at VVD Dronten are not proud of this. We take responsibility for hospitably accommodating Ukrainians and the reception center. Now it is time for other municipalities to take responsibility for emergency shelter.

112990.explanation: Anyone who likes to enjoy the outdoors in the cold can, according to VVD Dronten, simply enjoy a terrace heater. Whether at home under the veranda or on the terrace at our hospitality venues.

112991.explanation: It is important that there is support in society for a fireworks show. This must first be researched and asked about in advance. If there is sufficient support, it can be organized. Preferably per village!

112992.explanation: Keeping neighborhoods and our rural areas clean and safe is a priority. Illegal dumping is heavily fined. VVD Dronten especially wants to prevent bulky waste from ending up in nature. Inhabitants should also be relieved as much as possible in disposing of waste.

112996.explanation: It is important to have local news. It's nice to see the many initiatives in this area, from DeDrontenaar, De Dronter Reporter to Lokale Omroep Dronten. Support for these great initiatives should mainly come from listeners and businesses.

113002.explanation: Camera surveillance will be used where necessary. Camera surveillance increases the chances of catching offenders and provides monitoring of risk areas. The center of Dronten calls for permanent camera surveillance to combat nuisance. Mobile camera surveillance will also be used more often.

113003.explanation: Every form of domestic violence is one too many and must be tackled severely. Therefore, it is important that perpetrators are removed from the home, not the victims!

113004.explanation: VVD Dronten takes our safety seriously. We have a beautiful municipality and want to keep it that way. Therefore, BOAs must be properly equipped, if necessary with heavier means of force: batons, pepper spray, camera surveillance and bodycams increase safety for both enforcers and residents.

113006.explanation: Social rental homes are only available for people who need them most. Therefore, we tackle misallocation of social housing. We want tenants of social rental homes to have the opportunity to buy their home, so they can build wealth. This stimulates turnover and enables OFW to build more.

113009.explanation: We also want flexible building solutions. Flexible homes are realized within six months to provide immediate solutions. Vacant offices and shops are converted into homes. VVD Dronten wants to stimulate housing splitting.

113010.explanation: Where the market does not take action, the municipality can (temporarily) play a role. We already see good examples of this in our municipality. Often, money invested by the municipality is also a requirement from national subsidies.

113011.explanation: Social assistance is a safety net for those who really need it. It is important that we provide this safety net, but we also ask for a counterperformance. With a counterperformance, you stay active: you meet new people and keep regularity in your life.

113012.explanation: VVD Dronten finds it important that everyone contributes to society. Taxes are not without reason. Residents with a low income receive support on many fronts. Also, if people hopefully soon earn a higher income, they must pay taxes.

113016.explanation: Traffic safety must be improved in some places in our municipality. Speed violators must be dealt with severely. Putting the entire built-up area at 30 km/h is not the solution. On some through roads, 50 km/h is safer.

113019.explanation: Everyone receives the care they need. This is done by professionals. According to VVD Dronten, faith, culture, or belief do not play a role in this.

113026.explanation: VVD Dronten has ensured that shops may open on Sundays just like supermarkets. We are proud of this. It is up to entrepreneurs to decide when they want to take advantage of this opportunity. We hope often!

113027.explanation: We do not believe there should be special priority for status holders for social rental housing. We find it important that there are equal opportunities for people who have long been waiting for a home.

113029.explanation: The landscape surrounding our municipality has been shaped by farmers over recent decades and is characteristic for our municipality. We are committed to preserving the agricultural area and cultural landscape in our municipality. We only agree if it benefits our municipality.

113031.explanation: VVD Dronten does not want to make school swimming compulsory. However, it is important that as many children as possible learn to swim. It is the parents' responsibility to ensure children learn to swim. The lesson packages are already full enough.

113032.explanation: National policy tolerates soft drugs, and so do we. At this moment, VVD Dronten takes a cautious stance regarding coffeeshops in the municipality of Dronten. Much also depends on the proposal and location of a coffeeshop.

113034.explanation: We want to allow this, but not always and everywhere. In Dronten, for example, the Silo and Watergraaf are examples where this worked well. In most places, we will be reserved because it must fit our municipality and the village character.

113036.explanation: There is scarcity on busy days. Also given the growth of our municipality, this is important. More customers also helps combat vacancy.

113038.explanation: Labor migrants make an important contribution to the business activity in our municipality. We provide good and safe housing for labor migrants, together with entrepreneurs. At locations around the campus, around the center, in industrial areas, and where necessary, customized solutions-with an eye for safety.

113040.explanation: Nuisance from low-flying areas should be prevented as much as possible. But VVD Dronten stands for a strong military and training must take place, which unfortunately causes nuisance. Many Dronten residents are or were active in the defense forces; we are proud of them!

113041.explanation: Drop-in centers form a permanent base in all three centers, if supported by the population. In recent years, VVD Dronten has committed itself to community centers such as De Kans. It is important that volunteers, entrepreneurs, and residents remain involved.

112980.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten wants diversity and equal opportunities but does not impose a mandatory 50/50 split. They strive for a balanced distribution based on quality and inclusion, not hard quotas.

112981.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten wants residents to think along and have influence, but not via referenda. The election program opts for other forms such as the Dronter Future Council, Youth Panel, advisory boards, and digital consultation hours to take decisions together.

112984.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten wants room for hospitality but also peace and livability. Opening hours should not be completely free. The party opts for tailor-made solutions where the interests of entrepreneurs and residents are weighed to prevent nuisance.

112985.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten finds this very important. Generating energy together strengthens the community. Proceeds can go to residents, new sustainable projects, or social initiatives such as a community garden, meeting place, or support for sports clubs. This way, the money stays local.

112986.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten wants a strong, sustainable local economy and no monotonous block buildings. New distribution centers cause lots of traffic with all its downsides and few jobs. The party chooses variation, quality, and companies that contribute to work, sustainability, and livability.

112987.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten wants everyone in our municipality to have opportunities to participate. Refugees are part of this. Closing shelters goes against their values of solidarity and inclusion. They opt for protection, language, work, and a municipality where everyone feels welcome.

112990.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to save energy and stimulate climate-friendly choices. Terrace heating uses a lot of energy and contributes to CO₂ emissions. Banning it helps prevent waste. Cosiness is possible without heating, for example with blankets or indoor spaces.

112991.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes a fireworks show is not a municipal task. If residents or entrepreneurs want to organize a show, the municipality can assist with safety and sustainability. Alternatives are light shows, laser shows, or music parties. This keeps it fun without extra costs.

112992.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants waste policy to be social and sustainable. Free collection sounds attractive, but costs a lot and can lead to more waste. They believe it should remain affordable and accessible, with alternatives such as a well-functioning recycling center and shared collection actions for those who cannot bring waste themselves.

112996.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds local media important for democracy and involvement. Extra support helps inform residents well and let them participate. This keeps the broadcaster independent and accessible to everyone in Dronten.

113002.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants residents to feel safe, but more cameras are not always the solution. They prefer prevention, youth work, and visible BOAs. Camera surveillance can be used in exceptional situations but only with clear rules and protection of privacy.

113003.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants victims of domestic violence to receive help quickly and safely. There is currently too little shelter in Flevoland. Therefore, they believe the municipality should realize a women’s shelter together with partners, so no one has to remain unprotected.

113004.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants BOAs to be visible and accessible to help residents. Safety arises through prevention, cooperation, and trust, not by more weapons.

113006.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA understands the desire for priority, but there are more groups with urgent housing needs, such as people in emergency situations or with medical problems. More important is investing in sufficient affordable homes for everyone.

113009.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to combat vacancy and realize more affordable housing. Converting offices into homes with municipal support helps make better use of space and contributes to a sustainable and social housing solution.

113010.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA does not see a major shortage of student housing in Dronten. Furthermore, students mostly prefer to live outside the campus. Temporary container homes are therefore not a priority.

113011.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants people on social assistance to get perspective, not forced work. Coercion does not help; guidance, education, and basic jobs give people a fair chance to participate in a way that suits their situation.

113012.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants people with low incomes not to be additionally taxed. Waiving municipal taxes helps secure livelihoods and prevents poverty. Stopping this would further endanger vulnerable residents.

113016.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten wants tailored solutions. In busy areas, 30 km/h is better for safety and livability. On other roads, 50 km/h can remain. It depends on the situation and what is safest and most practical.

113019.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten values freedom of choice, but in a small municipality this is not always feasible. We do ensure that everyone is helped respectfully and appropriately, with attention to culture and beliefs where possible.

113026.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten wants freedom of choice for residents and entrepreneurs. Sunday openings can help with busy schedules and the local economy. We do pay attention to neighborhood peace and good agreements about working hours and staff.

113027.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Dronten wants people with residence permits to participate quickly. Priority on social housing helps them gain a secure base. This prevents homelessness, overcrowded asylum centers, and promotes integration in our community.

113029.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants solar panels on roofs, not on agricultural land. This keeps fertile soil available for food production and protects nature and landscapes.

113031.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants all children to be able to play sports and be active, regardless of income. School swimming supports safety and health. This fits with their commitment to equal opportunities and supporting youth and sports in the municipality.

113032.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to allow a coffeeshop to take cannabis cultivation and sales out of the grey market. This promotes safety, prevents crime, and provides clarity for users. The condition is that it is well regulated and fits within national frameworks.

113034.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants mainly affordable and sustainable housing in mixed neighborhoods. High-rise buildings do not fit the village character and livability of Dronten. Growth must be careful, with space for greenery and social interaction, not high-rise construction.

113036.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA does not want extra parking spaces in the center. There are already enough spots. More parking means more car traffic and less space for greenery and social interaction. The party prefers cycling and public transport.

113038.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants labor migrants to have good housing and be protected against exploitation. More homes are needed to prevent abuses and give people a safe place. This is part of a fair and social municipality.

113040.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to protect peace, health, and nature. They oppose low-flying zones and military helicopters over nature areas and villages. This prevents noise nuisance and damage to the living environment.

113041.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to structurally support walk-in centers. They are important for meeting people and providing help in the neighborhood. Extra funding helps ensure continuity and quality, so everyone has a place to turn to.

112980.explanation: It is about having the right people in the right places. Women and men are equal in this. The Christian Union considers it important that equal treatment is applied here.

112981.explanation: Council members are elected to weigh all interests and make well-considered decisions. Residents should be able to assume that council members do this carefully and perform the work for which they have been mandated. The Christian Union supports a corrective referendum for residents.

112984.explanation: Cafes and restaurants are connected to places where people come together, live together and socialize. We find this important. This also includes healthy coordination so that residents can reasonably enjoy a good night's rest.

112985.explanation: The Christian Union welcomes citizen initiatives but this does not always mean the municipality must financially support them. Support from the municipality can also be visible by providing advice.

112986.explanation: Within Dronten, several industrial areas are suitable for distribution centers. Considering the preservation of the village character of the municipality, careful consideration must be given to the maximum capacity the municipality can handle to avoid problems.

112987.explanation: The Christian Union regards caring for neighbors as a great value. We take responsibility within the municipality by caring for people applying for asylum.

112990.explanation: The Christian Union assumes that everyone takes responsibility for the sustainable use of energy.

112991.explanation: Firework shows incur high costs and are not necessarily environmentally friendly. When a national fireworks ban (which the Christian Union supports) is implemented, this can be reconsidered.

112992.explanation: It is important that residents can more easily participate in waste separation. There are sufficient arrangements from the municipality to dispose of bulky waste. If residents need help, it is important that the municipality is easily accessible for assistance.

112996.explanation: The Christian Union believes that extra money is not always the answer. Moreover, there are many information channels available for residents to use.

113002.explanation: There are always more wishes than available funds. The municipal council must carefully weigh the various wishes, needs, and interests. Where safety is demonstrably at risk due to the absence of cameras, the Christian Union wants to consider this.

113003.explanation: A safe living environment for children. Child abuse and domestic violence must be a priority in municipal policy. Agencies like Safe at Home must be able to do their work properly, with sufficient resources.

113004.explanation: The Christian Union follows national policy on this. When changes occur in police equipment, these also apply to the enforcement officers insofar as they have access to them.

113006.explanation: Out of solidarity and compassion, we want to commit in Dronten to building affordable owner-occupied and rental homes for starters, others with a small income, and people with care needs. At least 30% of new construction will be social rental housing. This applies to residents from both inside and outside the municipality.

113009.explanation: We actively look at what is possible to add housing for our residents. Think of splitting large existing homes or redeveloping industrial areas.

113010.explanation: The Christian Union Dronten wants to revive campus housing at Aeres University of Applied Sciences so that there will be and remain housing available for students.

113011.explanation: Our social security system is intended as a safety net for times when life is difficult: in case of unemployment, illness, disability, or old age. The Christian Union assumes that everyone ultimately wants to contribute to society (paid or unpaid).

113012.explanation: The Christian Union believes that a fair claim must be made on people. If they have difficulties with payments, an appropriate solution must be found. We all contribute to society through taxes and ability to pay.

113016.explanation: The maximum speed within built-up areas will be 30 kilometers per hour. Only on roads where currently 50 km/h is allowed and public transport uses them will 50 km/h apply. In addition, there will be more residential zones where no faster than 15 kilometers per hour is allowed.

113019.explanation: Every person has the right to choose care that fits their life convictions and meaning.

113026.explanation: Shopkeepers may not be forced to open their store on Sundays. Dronten must guarantee and monitor this. The Christian Union is against further relaxation of Sunday opening hours: calm on Sunday mornings for churchgoers remains.

113027.explanation: Our municipality also takes responsibility for housing asylum seekers with residence status and strives to find housing for them. Anyone who does not leave behind housing has the right to priority.

113029.explanation: The Christian Union believes that first good consideration must be given to possibilities for placing solar panels on roofs, parking lots, and other places where solar panels can be functionally installed.

113031.explanation: The Christian Union believes that through the Dronten Pass a lasting contribution is made to teaching primary school children how to swim. Obtaining a diploma should be possible for everyone.

113032.explanation: The social norm must return that drug use is bad and undesirable. The Christian Union supports healthy growing up in healthy conditions, which does not include a coffeeshop.

113034.explanation: The Christian Union wants a village character with housing up to a maximum of five stories. Building higher disrupts the village character of the municipality of Dronten.

113036.explanation: The Christian Union wants cleaner transport, with walking, cycling, and public transport as most important. Therefore, we choose the STOMP principle: First Steps, then Pedaling (bike), then Public transport, then Mobility-as-a-Service and only then the Passenger car. This makes many parking spaces unnecessary.

113038.explanation: The Christian Union (CU) does not want large-scale housing for labor migrants. Large-scale housing for labor migrants – 150 places or more – will be banned; this does not fit with Dronten, but rather dispersed and small-scale locations inside and outside the centers.

113040.explanation: The CU Dronten adheres to national policy and considers defense necessary given the current circumstances.

113041.explanation: The CU believes that if there is a reason to increase funding, this should happen through dialogue and consultation with the municipality, other social partners, and De Kans. The CU warmly supports De Kans with appropriate, systemic support from the municipality if needed.

112980.explanation: Diversity in governance is important. Quality and representation go hand in hand, without rigid quotas.

112981.explanation: Not talking about but with our residents is very important for D66. We believe in a corrective binding referendum. This way, the people of Dronten can participate in important decisions.

112984.explanation: Although D66 would like entrepreneurs to have as much freedom as possible to make their own choices, late opening hours can lead to nighttime disturbances. This must be balanced. Residents should not be the victims of late opening hours.

112985.explanation: The energy transition does not happen automatically. Where residents want to use the electricity they consume and generate more intelligently, this should be encouraged.

112986.explanation: D66 is not fundamentally against the arrival of distribution centers. However, D66 believes new companies should fit with Dronten’s agricultural identity and contribute to improving our local economy.

112987.explanation: Proper shelter for refugees is a shared responsibility. Dronten contributes to this and stands for humane reception with attention to support and safety.

112990.explanation: Terrace heating is hardly used in Dronten currently. Entrepreneurs bear their own responsibility in this. However, we encourage sustainable alternatives and energy savings.

112991.explanation: A central fireworks show can ensure more safety, less nuisance, and less damage, and is of course simply fun and a moment of meeting.

112992.explanation: The current system encourages reuse and waste separation. Completely free collection can lead to more waste and higher costs. Additionally, D66 believes in the 'polluter pays' principle. For small waste, people in all villages can use the mobile recycling station (which is free).

112996.explanation: Local media play an important role in democracy and information provision. Extra support can contribute to a strong, independent local broadcaster.

113002.explanation: Camera surveillance is not an end in itself but can temporarily contribute to safety at specific problem locations, alongside prevention and other measures.

113003.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be able to access help quickly and safely. Good shelter facilities are essential, but according to D66, this does not necessarily have to be in Dronten. Cooperation within the Flevoland region and ensuring enough shelter places there is important.

113004.explanation: Enforcers must be able to do their job safely, but de-escalating actions are paramount. Arming officers is not a structural solution to safety issues.

113006.explanation: Residents with ties to Dronten must have a fair chance at housing, within legal possibilities.

113009.explanation: Transformation can help with housing shortages but depends on location, quality, and feasibility for each building. Entrepreneurs must ensure this is financially viable without municipal subsidies.

113010.explanation: Housing construction in Dronten must focus on sustainable solutions for residents. Temporary container housing is not a structural solution and does not meet students’ wishes either. There are sufficient student accommodations around Aeres. Temporary housing is therefore not needed.

113011.explanation: Participation in society is important, but reciprocal obligations must be appropriate, voluntary, and not stigmatizing.

113012.explanation: Debt remission prevents people with low incomes from getting further into trouble. Solidarity is important, and financial situations should not cause inequality of opportunity.

113016.explanation: Lower speeds lead to greater traffic safety, less noise nuisance, and a more pleasant living environment in residential neighborhoods.

113019.explanation: People should be able to choose care that fits their background and beliefs, as long as the quality of care is guaranteed.

113026.explanation: Entrepreneurs and residents in Dronten can choose whether to open on shopping Sundays. Shopping Sundays provide flexibility and vibrancy in the center.

113027.explanation: Everyone must have a fair chance of getting a home. The solution mainly lies in building more (for specific groups seeking housing), not in pitting groups against each other.

113029.explanation: Sustainable energy is important, but our agricultural lands are too valuable and require protection. There is still plenty of space on roofs and industrial estates.

113031.explanation: Swimming safety is important. Every child must be able to learn to swim, regardless of their financial situation at home. However, this does not have to be taught at school. Parents can organize this themselves. Parents who cannot afford it can use the funds on the Dronten Pass.

113032.explanation: Under strict conditions, a coffeeshop can contribute to regulation, safety, and reducing illegal trade.

113034.explanation: For D66, the future vision is leading, where a maximum of five stories is allowed. Only with good reasons can deviations be made. This fits the village character of our towns.

113036.explanation: The center of Dronten has sufficient parking spaces. Good accessibility is important, but more parking spaces have not proven necessary.

113038.explanation: Good and sufficient housing prevents nuisance and exploitation. Labor migrants working here deserve decent living conditions.

113040.explanation: National security is important, but plans must be carefully weighed to prevent nuisance and not harm local livability and nature.

113041.explanation: Low-threshold meeting places play an important role in welfare and prevention. The goal should be to support this and ensure these opportunities exist in Dronten, not to specifically support one initiative. D66 supports walk-in facilities but not necessarily De Kans.

112980.explanation: Quality comes first, diversity is important but no hard quotas. Political parties have a great responsibility here themselves.

112981.explanation: In big decisions, residents must be able to express themselves directly. This strengthens trust in local democracy.

112984.explanation: (Especially small) entrepreneurs deserve space. At the same time, livability for residents is taken into account. Tailored solutions per area.

112985.explanation: Citizen initiatives that contribute to affordability and sustainability deserve support.

112986.explanation: Employment is welcome, but spatial quality and traffic pressure are decisive. Furthermore, this employment should primarily benefit locally. This is often not the case.

112987.explanation: We take our responsibility, but reception must remain appropriate to the scale and capacity of Dronten.

112990.explanation: We encourage sustainability, but a total ban is too rigid. Together we seek feasible alternatives.

112991.explanation: The national government seemingly leaves firework enforcement to the municipalities again. Firework displays are allowed, but only if they contribute to safety and have community support. More important are safety and livability. Once again, municipalities are left in the lurch.

112992.explanation: Accessibility is important, but costs must be fairly distributed. As long as there is no fair alternative, this kind of collective solution remains preferable.

112996.explanation: Independent media are important. Supporting local media is essential.

113002.explanation: Deployment should be targeted only at concrete problems. No standard solution. It is a last resort. There is still much to gain from prevention.

113003.explanation: Victims of violence must have nearby and safe access. These matters are always handled regionally, which is also sensible. It is important that these facilities are well accessible and easily approachable for our residents.

113004.explanation: Safety requires professionalism, but arming personnel is not a standard solution. We are also concerned about a negative spiral. A stronger focus on prevention is strongly preferred.

113006.explanation: Social cohesion is important, but rules must be legally sound.

113009.explanation: Transformation is desirable, but subsidies only if they contribute to affordable housing.

113010.explanation: Student housing is realistically not a real problem. This is indicated by the university of applied sciences and the OFW. We therefore prefer to focus on first-time buyers on the housing market.

113011.explanation: We encourage participation, but always with customization and respect.

113012.explanation: People with low incomes must be protected.

113016.explanation: Often logical in residential neighborhoods, but not automatically everywhere.

113019.explanation: Freedom of choice is important. The harsh reality, however, is that availability is often leading.

113026.explanation: Entrepreneurs and residents should have freedom here. We believe the interests of small entrepreneurs should have priority. Opening should be an option, not an obligation.

113027.explanation: Here we must follow the legal agreements, taking into account the waiting times of others.

113029.explanation: Protecting agricultural land is important, but customization remains necessary here.

113031.explanation: Swimming skills are essential for safety.

113032.explanation: There has been demand for this for years. The police and social welfare organizations also request this. All of this only within clear safety frameworks and with local support.

113034.explanation: Densification may be necessary, but must fit the environment. The future vision created by the residents is leading for us: no high-rise flats.

113038.explanation: Decent housing is needed, but not at the expense of regular housing construction. Employers bear primary responsibility here.

113040.explanation: The livability and peace of residents come first. At the same time, this lies outside the municipality's influence and politics must be honest about that.

113041.explanation: Inloophuis de Kans is no longer a partner. Easily accessible meeting places strengthen social cohesion, so the municipality must continue investing here. It is important to invest cohesively with all welfare partners.

statements.title: More housing for migrant workers should be built in Dronten.

titleParts.text: In Dronten moeten meer woningen voor

titleParts.glossary: A person who comes to the Netherlands from another country temporarily to work.

statements.title: Residents of Dronten should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Dronten moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Dronten.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should oppose the army's plans to make Dronten a low-flying area for helicopters.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipal enforcement officers (boa's) who monitor safety in Dronten should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Dronten controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
There should be a women's shelter in Dronten.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: A movable and temporary dwelling, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: More tall residential apartment blocks should be allowed in Dronten.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should invest more money in the drop-in center De Kans in Biddinghuizen.
